title: Rich Roasted Peruvian Potato Hash
description: A salty, aerated, brunch-mood Peruvian dish featuring ketchup and ferment-kimchi, with a Mediterranean twist.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 400 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a large bowl, toss the diced potatoes with 1 tablespoon of olive oil, salt, and black pepper until they are evenly coated.
Spread the potatoes out in a single layer on a baking sheet and roast for 20 minutes.
While the potatoes are roasting, heat the remaining 1 tablespoon of olive oil in a large skillet over medium heat.
Add the diced red onion to the skillet and cook until it is softened and translucent.
Add the minced garlic to the skillet and cook for an additional 1 minute.
Stir in the chopped ferment-kimchi and cook for 2 minutes.
Once the potatoes are done roasting, add them to the skillet and stir to combine with the onion and kimchi mixture.
Use a spatula to press the potato mixture down into the skillet and cook for an additional 2 minutes to crisp the bottom.
Stir in the crumbly feta cheese and cook until it is melted and well combined.
Serve the hash hot, topped with a dollop of ketchup and a sprinkle of chopped fresh cilantro.
